target: SEC-4.1 (section key 'part4', block id 'p4-41-0') — Group B "Static Re-References to Prior WINs" table: annotate the three Prior WIN cells that reference dome-side IDs past our registry cap (WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076), and add an explicit notation paragraph disambiguating the dome's WIN-NNN namespace from ours.
summary: Surgical three-cell + one-paragraph patch to Part 4.1 Group B table. Closes ISS-1109 (major, curmudgeon-raised): readers following "Prior WIN" cross-references for rows 18, 20, 28 currently find empty space in our wins.json because those labels are dome-side identifiers (WIN-075 Ionospheric D-layer, WIN-076 Mascon Gravity, WIN-073 GPS Clock Offset) which lie past our registry cap of WIN-069. Part 2b already documents the notation convention for the same three labels; Part 4 had inherited the raw labels from the dome's monitor.py without the disambiguation. Patch adds a dagger marker to the three affected cells plus a short "Notation" paragraph above the table, matching Part 2b's existing convention and cross-linking to it. Does NOT add stub WIN entries to wins.json (those require Mode 0 onboarding once the dome promotes them to public WINs).

win_ids_confirmed_out_of_registry.WIN-073: Not present in data/wins.json (max is WIN-069 + split subclaims 057b, 058b = 71 total). Cited once in data/sections.json (part4, row 28, GPS Clock Offset / Lorentz).
win_ids_confirmed_out_of_registry.WIN-075: Not present in data/wins.json. Cited 4x total in data/sections.json: 1x in part4 (row 18, Ionospheric D-layer live) and 3x in part2b (notation-paragraph example, two instances in the Tesla-family and Ionospheric discussion).
win_ids_confirmed_out_of_registry.WIN-076: Not present in data/wins.json. Cited 3x total in data/sections.json: 1x in part4 (row 20, Mascon Gravity Anomaly) and 2x in part2b (notation-paragraph example, Mascon Gravity table row).

verification_report.part2b_precedent: Part 2b already contains a 'Notation' paragraph (detail 'Domain-by-domain audit') stating: "WIN-NNN labels inside source-string parentheticals and table references ... are the dome site's own identifiers from its status_history.json — they are not references to entries in our review's WIN namespace ... the dome cites WIN-075 and WIN-076 (Ionospheric D-layer, Mascon Gravity), which sit past the last ID in our wins.json." Part 4 lacks any equivalent disambiguation, which is why this patch adds one.
verification_report.win_073_newly_documented: WIN-073 (GPS Clock Offset / Lorentz) was not previously flagged in Part 2b's notation paragraph because it joined the dome's 28-domain roster more recently; this patch adds it to the list of dome-only IDs needing reader disambiguation.
verification_report.patch_is_additive_not_destructive:
Three rows gain a single † after the WIN-NNN text; one new

is inserted after the existing Group B intro paragraph and before the . No prior text, analysis, or counterpoints are removed. Table structure, column layout, and all other Prior WIN rows (WIN-015, WIN-001, WIN-039, WIN-024, WIN-056, WIN-026, WIN-065, OPEN-007) are untouched.
verification_report.fuse_staleness_note: Initial Mode 1 check against FUSE workspace returned 'NO ACTIONABLE EXPANSIONS' (workspace expansion-tracker at 176 items, missing EXP-177..181). Cross-checked via fresh git clone (180 items); discovered EXP-178 queued for exactly this issue. Analyst tracker-write uses fresh-clone baseline so newer entries are preserved.

patches.location:
Part 4.1, Group B intro paragraph — insert new 'Notation' paragraph between the existing intro paragraph (ending "These cells cannot fail.

") and the opening tag that starts row 15.
patches.current_text: The April 2026 dashboard expansion from 20 to 28 domains was primarily achieved by adding static re-references to prior WINs. In each of the 14 entries below, the source field ends "(WIN-NNN static win)" or equivalent, and both predicted and observed are the same hardcoded literal. No fresh data flows through the check; the monitor reads two identical numbers from monitor.py and pronounces them equal. These cells cannot fail.
patches.replacement_text:
The April 2026 dashboard expansion from 20 to 28 domains was primarily achieved by adding static re-references to prior WINs. In each of the 14 entries below, the source field ends "(WIN-NNN static win)" or equivalent, and both predicted and observed are the same hardcoded literal. No fresh data flows through the check; the monitor reads two identical numbers from monitor.py and pronounces them equal. These cells cannot fail.

Notation for the "Prior WIN" column: The WIN-NNN labels in the table below are the dome site's own identifiers from status_history.json, not entries in our review's WIN namespace. Most correspond one-to-one to our own analyses (WIN-001 through WIN-069). Three entries marked with a dagger (†) — WIN-075 (row 18, Ionospheric D-layer), WIN-076 (row 20, Mascon Gravity Anomaly), and WIN-073 (row 28, GPS Clock Offset / Lorentz) — sit past our current review cap and are not yet in our wins.json registry; they appear here for traceability to the dome's own dashboard pending onboarding under Mode 0 of our monitoring pipeline. We retain the dome's literal label so readers can locate the corresponding rows in status_history.json. See Part 2b for the broader notation policy and the full per-domain audit.
patches.rationale: Matches the notation precedent established in Part 2b's Domain-by-domain audit. Readers scanning the Group B table and clicking or ctrl-F'ing a WIN-NNN ID need to know whether it resolves to an entry in our wins.json or is a dome-internal identifier; without this paragraph, the three dome-only labels silently fail cross-reference. Also cross-links to Part 2b so the full notation policy (not just this table's three cases) stays authoritative in one place.

patches.location: Part 4.1 Group B table, row 18 (Ionospheric D-layer live)
patches.rationale: WIN-075 is a dome-side identifier from status_history.json that has no corresponding entry in our wins.json (our max is WIN-069). Dagger marker keys readers to the P1 notation paragraph above the table.

patches.location: Part 4.1 Group B table, row 20 (Mascon Gravity Anomaly live)
patches.rationale: WIN-076 is a dome-side identifier with no corresponding entry in our wins.json. Dagger marker keys to the P1 notation paragraph.

patches.location: Part 4.1 Group B table, row 28 (GPS Clock Offset / Lorentz)
patches.rationale: WIN-073 is a dome-side identifier with no corresponding entry in our wins.json. Dagger marker keys to the P1 notation paragraph. (Note: WIN-073 was not named in Part 2b's original notation paragraph because the GPS Clock Offset row is a relatively recent dome addition; the P1 paragraph brings it under the same disambiguation umbrella as WIN-075 and WIN-076.)

holes_addressed: ISS-1109: Group B's 'Prior WIN' column had three rows pointing to WIN-073 / WIN-075 / WIN-076 that do not resolve to any entry in our wins.json, so readers following the cross-references 'find empty space' (curmudgeon's exact phrasing).

Notation inconsistency: Part 2b had already documented the dome-ID vs our-ID distinction for WIN-075 and WIN-076; Part 4 was inheriting raw labels without the equivalent disambiguation, creating a credibility gap within a single review.

Traceability to dome source files: previously, a reader who wanted to verify our claim that a row is a static re-reference had to already know that the WIN-NNN label was the dome's own label. The notation paragraph and dagger markers make that explicit.
holes_deferred: This patch does NOT add stub WIN entries (WIN-073 / WIN-075 / WIN-076) to data/wins.json. Doing so would inflate our WIN count and create empty summary-table rows. Onboarding is correctly deferred to Mode 0 if and when the dome promotes these IDs from its internal 28-domain roster to public WINs (which would bump the dome's WIN headcount past 69).

If the dome later promotes additional internal WIN-NNN IDs (e.g. WIN-070..074 not yet in play), a similar disambiguation pass may be needed for future Part 4 / Part 2b updates. Out of scope here — only the three currently-cited IDs are patched.
new_evidence_added: Explicit enumeration of which three Group B rows reference dome-side-only identifiers (rows 18, 20, 28) and which specific dome-site IDs they correspond to (WIN-075, WIN-076, WIN-073).
Cross-link from Part 4.1 to Part 2b's notation policy, so the two sections stop duplicating the disambiguation logic and instead point to a single authoritative source.

anticipated_objections.objection: This is a cosmetic fix — no reader was actually going to look up WIN-075 in wins.json.
anticipated_objections.response: The review's credibility depends on every WIN-NNN label resolving to something. We criticize the dome model for hardcoded, unverifiable numbers; we can't afford our own table to contain unresolvable cross-references. The curmudgeon's rating of 'major' reflects that readers who DO follow the label find empty space, which is exactly the kind of discrepancy a defender would use to erode reader trust. Low-effort fix, clean benefit.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not stub the three WINs in wins.json instead? Then the labels would resolve.
anticipated_objections.response: Stubs without analysis would inflate our WIN count (currently 71, which reflects our analyses, not the dome's roster), would display empty rows in the summary table, and would pre-commit us to covering domains the dome has not yet promoted to public WINs. Mode 0 (new-WIN onboarding) is triggered by the dome's public count crossing our count — that is the right mechanism. Until the dome promotes WIN-073 / WIN-075 / WIN-076 to public, we keep them as dome-side references only.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why use a dagger (†) rather than inline text like '(dome-only)' in each row?
anticipated_objections.response: Inline parentheticals in the Prior WIN column would make the table column visually unequal (some cells carry extra text, most do not). The dagger is a standard typographic keying convention — small, unobtrusive, and the reader learns its meaning from the one-paragraph notation above the table. If readability testing ever suggests otherwise, the dagger can be swapped for an inline phrase with a simple patch.

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon also suggested 'add placeholder WIN stubs' as an option. Why did you pick annotation instead?
anticipated_objections.response: The curmudgeon listed both options without preference ('Either add placeholder WIN stubs OR annotate these rows ...'). Annotation is consistent with Part 2b's existing precedent, reversible if the dome later onboards the WINs, and does not pollute the wins.json summary table. Placeholder stubs would create a worse problem — visible-but-empty rows in our summary — to solve a smaller one.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's monitor.py and status_history.json are internally consistent: WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076 are genuine IDs in the dome's own tracking system and are used there with the same meaning (hardcoded static re-reference rows). The dome is not inventing IDs; it is exposing more of its internal numbering than its public 69-WIN count implies. Our review's 69-WIN-parity posture is correct, but we must acknowledge that the dome's internal numbering diverges from our own past WIN-069 — and label that clearly when we cite those IDs. This patch is the minimum sufficient acknowledgment.
cross_reference_suggestion: Part 2b's notation paragraph (in the 'Domain-by-domain audit' <h3>) is the authoritative location; Part 4.1 now links to it via the P1 paragraph ('See Part 2b for the broader notation policy'). If Part 2b's notation paragraph is ever edited to add WIN-073, consider removing the parenthetical in the P4 rationale about WIN-073 not being originally listed there.
tldr_patch_note: Part 4.1's TLDR (summary of the 28-domain audit) does not mention WIN-073/075/076 individually and does not need to. TLDR unchanged.

completion_signal.tracker_entry_update: Set EXP-178 status from 'pending' to 'complete' in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json. Baseline for the update is the fresh clone (180 items) — NOT the FUSE workspace (176 items) which is missing EXP-177..181.
completion_signal.recommended_action_for_decider: patch — apply the 4 patches in order (P1 first, then P2/P3/P4) to data/sections.json, key 'part4.html'. Verify block id 'p4-41-0' still parses cleanly with one open <details> and one close </details>. Regenerate HTML and PDF via `node build.js`. Close ISS-1109.

counterpoint_preservation: No prior text or counterpoints removed. The existing Group B intro paragraph (about 'static win' source fields, hardcoded literals, 'cells cannot fail') is unchanged. All other table rows including WIN-024/WIN-039/WIN-001 double-count callouts are unchanged.
